title: NGE Koutetsu no Girlfriend Special Edition
display_title: NGE Koutetsu no Girlfriend Special Edition
wikitext:
[]: NGE: Koutetsu no Girlfriend Special Edition             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Wed Jan 02, 2008 11:54 pm Girlfriend of Steel, Special Edition. I can't read Chinese, but I assume its pretty much the same game. Since I haven't played the original, I will probably order it. Discuss. SAUCE: http://www.play-asia.com/paOS-13-71-7s-49-en-70-2h4f.html

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Thu Jan 03, 2008 4:54 am Three words - Gainax Cash Cow Looks lik Rebuild's bringing back all the old properties.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Feb 08, 2008 1:42 pm So they released the Special Edition in Chinese, huh? Cool, cool.

[]: Re: NGE: Koutetsu no Girlfriend Special Edition             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Feb 08, 2008 9:29 pm Wevangeliwon wrote: I can't read Chinese

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jan 16, 2012 6:11 am *bumping the old thread (actually there are at least two threads regarding GoS SV)* Well, I've finally played this game after months, I've been postponing it from some months. The new scenes are easily recognasible, mainly because of the different drawings, they've probably been made by Cyberfront rather than Gainax, but the more amazing part was Mana's voice during the flashback scene, she almost sounded like Yui. I've played the basic "Mana route" (I trusted Mana and then I kept searching her after the destruction of Raiden), and in my case the additional videos were apparently mere additions, they didn't require any special action/choice from my part, I've used the same route several times with the old GoS, I wonder if there are different endings or special events.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 16, 2012 9:17 am Special Edition has a brand new ending you can choose from. When Shinji's sitting on the bench brooding on what to do now, the original 3 ending choices are there, but there should be a new forth one at the bottom. You select that one then it shows Shinji and Mana reuniting in Tokyo-3 and apparently moving in together despite Angels still attacking. There's some other stuff going on too but I'm pretty sure I mentioned it in this or another thread. But yes, any decisions you make during the gameplay doesn't affect the ending you get, you can still choose whichever one you want.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jan 18, 2012 7:29 am ^thanks for mentioning it, SSD, I completely missed it. It's a quite... confusing ending, I'd say. It looks like Shinji, ahem, becomes a man, I never imagined to see a thing like that in an official publication. Also Asuka living together with Kaji, Hyuga declaring his love for Misato... it's really a weird ending, though Shinji drinking coffees like they were alcohol was probably the weirdest part.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 18, 2012 11:22 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: ^thanks for mentioning it, SSD, I completely missed it. It's a quite... confusing ending, I'd say. It looks like Shinji, ahem, becomes a man, I never imagined to see a thing like that in an official publication. Also Asuka living together with Kaji, Hyuga declaring his love for Misato...

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jan 18, 2012 11:32 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Yeah, very bizarre all around. To be honest (and no I'm NOT just saying this because Mana/Shinji hook up in that ending) , I feel the original three endings fit the tone better of the game, they have that bittersweet Eva feel to it, you know?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 18, 2012 12:03 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: I agree, it's like they turned the game into a "comedy" like GoS2nd but it feels not really adequate to GoS general tone.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jan 18, 2012 12:20 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Agreed. I mean, one thing that could have helped is NOT casually brushing off the fact the Angels are still attacking. If the Mana/Shinji ending was slightly adjusted to more of a tone of "There will still be battles since I'm an Eva pilot, but I want to stay by your side and protect you, Mana.", that would have been nice (though cliched, yeah)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 18, 2012 12:28 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: at least they should make sense inside their own continuity, adding that new ending to GoS was really weird.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jan 18, 2012 12:39 pm Sorry, I've been editing my previous post like a crazy, anyway I've not changed anything of major. Sailor Star Dust wrote: Well, GoS2 is RiAO not "our" Rei

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 18, 2012 12:42 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: it's really disappointing that they've not made a DVD Interactive porting for 2nd Impression too since I really liked it.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jan 18, 2012 12:49 pm ^I always thought that they never ported 2nd Impression because the first DVD wasn't a huge hit, a mere hypothesis of mine, though it's also true that those lazy guys never reprinted it and it's currently unavailable (my DVD is an used one, luckily in excellent conditions); your theory about the popularity of Mayumi is actually an interesting one, though probably we'll never know the exact reason, I just hope that sooner or later there'll be a porting of some kind.

[]:             Quote Post by ONIAgent150 » Tue Jan 24, 2012 12:38 am Hey, not to interrupt a discussion between the two veterans, but you all obviously know how to acquire some sort of English version of this. Does such a thing exist that you can let me know about? Or... No... I'll just... Sit over here... I really don't want to break any rules as a new member.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Jan 24, 2012 1:54 am ONIAgent150 wrote: I really don't want to break any rules as a new member.

[]:             Quote Post by ONIAgent150 » Tue Jan 24, 2012 2:14 pm Oh! Cool! Well, then is there a place where I can get an English version of the original? There is an AWFUL translation walkthrough on Youtube, but I was hoping to experience it for myself. Again, I don't want to break any rules, so if it's not legal, just hush me.
